proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients flagged unfit_for_intensive have a higher objective_response rate than fit patients in this AML cohort.
Patients with npm1_mutation=1 have a higher objective_response rate than NPM1 wild-type patients.
Patients with tp53_mutation=1 have a lower objective_response rate than TP53 wild-type patients.
Patients with complex_karyotype=1 have a lower objective_response rate than non-complex-karyotype patients.

analyses.result_summary: Unfit-for-intensive patients had a 22.10% objective response rate vs 16.51% for fit patients (n_unfit=20056). Chi-square p=1.88e-55. Direction supports hypothesis.
NPM1-mutated patients had 23.46% response vs 16.73% for NPM1 wild-type (n_npm1=15014). Chi-square p=5.79e-70. Direction supports hypothesis.
TP53-mutated patients had 16.57% response vs 18.99% for TP53 wild-type (n_tp53=4985). Chi-square p=3.51e-05. Direction supports hypothesis.
Complex-karyotype patients had 16.81% response vs 19.09% otherwise (n_ck=7462). Chi-square p=3.31e-06. Direction supports hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine have a higher objective_response rate than patients not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_7plus3 have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving 7+3 induction.
Patients receiving treatment_midostaurin have a different objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_gilteritinib have a different objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_ivosidenib have a different objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_enasidenib have a different objective_response rate than those not receiving it.

analyses.result_summary: Ven/Aza-treated 21.96% response vs 16.61% untreated (n_treated=20020). Chi-square p=6.62e-51. Strongly supports.
7+3-treated 18.89% response vs 18.63% untreated (n_treated=22580). Chi-square p=0.46. No detectable main effect.
Midostaurin-treated 18.57% response vs 18.78% untreated. Chi-square p=0.67. No detectable main effect.
Gilteritinib-treated 18.13% response vs 18.86% untreated. Chi-square p=0.14. No detectable main effect.
Ivosidenib-treated 18.82% response vs 18.74% untreated. Chi-square p=0.93. No main effect.
Enasidenib-treated 18.58% response vs 18.76% untreated. Chi-square p=0.79. No main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
Increasing age_years is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher probability of objective_response.
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
Higher wbc_k_per_ul is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate logistic: ecog_ps coefficient -0.353 per point (p=1.59e-90). Strongly supports negative direction.
age_years coef -0.0002 per year (p=0.88). No detectable univariate association with response.
albumin_g_dl coef +0.118 per g/dL (p=2.86e-07). Direction supports hypothesis.
weight_loss_pct_6mo coef -0.036 per percent (p=1.14e-30). Direction supports hypothesis.
blast_pct_marrow coef -0.005 per percent (p=2.41e-16). Direction supports hypothesis.
wbc_k_per_ul coef -0.0018 per K/uL (p=1.85e-04). Direction supports hypothesis.
crp_mg_l coef -0.0064 per mg/L (p=6.05e-06). Direction supports hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with secondary_aml=1 have a different objective_response rate than de novo AML patients.
Patients with flt3_itd=1 have a different objective_response rate than FLT3-ITD-negative patients.
Patients with idh1_mutation=1 have a different objective_response rate than IDH1 wild-type.
Patients with idh2_mutation=1 have a different objective_response rate than IDH2 wild-type.
Female patients (sex_female=1) have a different objective_response rate than male patients.

analyses.result_summary: Secondary AML 18.81% response vs 18.73% de novo (chi2 p=0.85). No detectable association.
FLT3-ITD 18.92% response vs 18.71% wild-type (chi2 p=0.64). No detectable main-effect association.
IDH1-mut 19.09% vs 18.72% wild-type (chi2 p=0.60). No detectable main-effect association.
IDH2-mut 19.15% vs 18.71% wild-type (chi2 p=0.47). No detectable main-effect association.
Female 18.91% vs male 18.62% (chi2 p=0.42). No detectable sex difference.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic model adjusting for all baseline features, the strongest independent positive predictors of objective_response are unfit_for_intensive, npm1_mutation, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine, and albumin_g_dl, while ecog_ps, weight_loss_pct_6mo, blast_pct_marrow, complex_karyotype, and tp53_mutation are independent negative predictors.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted logit coefficients (all p<0.001 unless noted): npm1_mutation +0.436, unfit_for_intensive +0.374, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine +0.362, albumin_g_dl +0.122; ecog_ps -0.362, weight_loss_pct_6mo -0.037, blast_pct_marrow -0.005, complex_karyotype -0.169, tp53_mutation -0.188, wbc_k_per_ul -0.0018, crp_mg_l -0.0065. Other treatments and labs not independently significant. Direction matches the hypothesis exactly.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_midostaurin increases objective_response specifically in flt3_itd=1 patients (drug-target interaction).
Treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response specifically in flt3_itd=1 patients.
Treatment_ivosidenib increases objective_response specifically in idh1_mutation=1 patients.
Treatment_enasidenib increases objective_response specifically in idh2_mutation=1 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef -0.106, p=0.19. In FLT3-ITD+: response 18.0% on midostaurin vs 19.4% off (TE=-0.015). Hypothesis NOT supported; no biomarker-driven benefit detected.
Interaction coef +0.079, p=0.32. In FLT3-ITD+: 18.7% on gilteritinib vs 18.6% off (TE=+0.002). Hypothesis NOT supported.
Interaction coef -0.304, p=0.077. In IDH1+: 15.4% on ivosidenib vs 19.5% off (TE=-0.040). Hypothesis NOT supported (point estimate is negative not positive).
Interaction coef +0.039, p=0.79. In IDH2+: 19.5% on enasidenib vs 19.1% off (TE=+0.004). Hypothesis NOT supported; effect indistinguishable from zero.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response more strongly in unfit_for_intensive=1 than in fit patients (positive treatment x unfit interaction).
Treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response more strongly in npm1_mutation=1 than NPM1 wild-type patients (positive treatment x NPM1 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef +0.883, p=1.14e-78. TE in unfit=1: +0.145; TE in unfit=0: -0.008. Strongly supports positive interaction.
Interaction coef +1.055, p=5.50e-102. TE in NPM1=1: +0.187; TE in NPM1=0: -0.004. Strongly supports positive interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is concentrated in patients who are BOTH npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1; the effect is small or zero in patients with only one or neither of these features.

analyses.code: Cross-tabulate ven_treatment effect by NPM1 x unfit cells
analyses.result_summary: Treatment effect of ven/aza by 4 cells: NPM1=0/unfit=0 TE=-0.010; NPM1=0/unfit=1 TE=+0.005; NPM1=1/unfit=0 TE=-0.003; NPM1=1/unfit=1 TE=+0.474 (n_t=2401, n_c=3585; 63.3% vs 16.0%). Chi-square within NPM1=1&unfit=1 cell p~0 (chi2 p=6.69e-310). Triple-interaction term ven*npm1*unfit coef=+2.13, p<0.001. Strongly supports.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the npm1_mutation=1 & unfit_for_intensive=1 favorable subgroup, presence of tp53_mutation=1 abolishes the venetoclax_azacitidine treatment effect.
Within the npm1_mutation=1 & unfit_for_intensive=1 favorable subgroup, presence of complex_karyotype=1 abolishes the venetoclax_azacitidine treatment effect.

analyses.code: Stratify ven TE within NPM1=1 & unfit=1 by TP53 status
analyses.result_summary: Within NPM1=1 & unfit=1: TP53=0 ven TE=+0.534 (n_t=2125 rr=0.691; n_c=3224 rr=0.157); TP53=1 ven TE=+0.006 (n_t=276 rr=0.188; n_c=361 rr=0.183). Effect entirely abolished by TP53 mutation. Globally, ven*tp53 interaction coef=-0.409, p=4.67e-07. Supports.

analyses.code: Stratify ven TE within NPM1=1 & unfit=1 by complex_karyotype status
analyses.result_summary: Within NPM1=1 & unfit=1: CK=0 ven TE=+0.556 (n_t=2025 rr=0.717; n_c=3052 rr=0.161); CK=1 ven TE=+0.032 (n_t=376 rr=0.184; n_c=533 rr=0.152). Effect abolished by complex karyotype. Globally, ven*CK interaction coef=-0.306, p=6.00e-06. Supports.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response by approximately +0.63 (78.6% vs 15.8%) specifically and only in patients meeting ALL FOUR predicates: npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0; in any subgroup violating any one of these predicates, the treatment effect is not different from zero.

analyses.code: Define favorable_subgroup = (npm1=1)&(unfit=1)&(tp53=0)&(CK=0); fit Logit(y ~ ven + favorable + ven:favorable). Also test cells where 1 predicate is violated.
analyses.result_summary: Favorable subgroup (n=4531, 9.1% of cohort): ven TE=+0.628 (rr_t=0.786 vs rr_c=0.158). Outside favorable: ven TE=-0.003. Subgroup-level interaction coef ven_x_fav=+2.99, p<1e-300. Effect dissolves on each violation: NPM1=0 (others fav) TE=+0.002; unfit=0 (others fav) TE=-0.004; TP53=1 (others fav) TE=+0.004; CK=1 (others fav) TE=+0.033. All four predicates jointly required.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the npm1=1 & unfit=1 & tp53=0 & CK=0 favorable subgroup, none of the other five treatments (treatment_midostaurin, treatment_gilteritinib, treatment_ivosidenib, treatment_enasidenib, treatment_7plus3) increases objective_response, ruling out treatment-confounding as the explanation for the venetoclax_azacitidine subgroup effect.

analyses.code: Within favorable subgroup, compute response rate by treatment receipt for each of the 6 treatments
analyses.result_summary: Within favorable subgroup (n=4531): midostaurin TE=-0.001, gilteritinib TE=-0.0004, ivosidenib TE=-0.019, enasidenib TE=+0.059, 7+3 TE=-0.004, ven/aza TE=+0.628. Only ven/aza shows a meaningful effect; others are near zero or small in either direction. Confirms ven/aza specificity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is no clinically large interaction between any treatment and continuous baseline features (age, ECOG, WBC, blast %, albumin, LDH, weight loss, CRP, NLR, hemoglobin) for objective_response.

analyses.code: For each treatment x continuous-feature pair, fit Logit(y ~ T + F + T*F) and report interaction p
analyses.result_summary: Of 6 treatments x 10 continuous features (60 tests), only two interactions reach p<0.01: treatment_enasidenib*hemoglobin_g_dl coef=-0.078, p=1.16e-03 (consistent with chance given multiple testing); treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine*blast_pct_marrow coef=+0.0035, p=3.52e-03 (small effect size). No large clinical interaction with continuous features detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among unfit_for_intensive=1 patients, ECOG performance status remains a strong negative predictor of objective_response independent of age.
Among AML patients, a constellation of cachexia/inflammation markers (low albumin, high CRP, high weight loss, high blast %) jointly predicts lower objective_response after adjustment.

analyses.code: Within unfit_for_intensive=1, fit Logit(y ~ age + ecog)
analyses.result_summary: Within unfit cohort (n=20056): ecog_ps coef -0.36 (p<0.001), age_years coef approximately +0.0 (p>0.5). ECOG dominates; age is not predictive within fitness category.

analyses.code: Multivariable logit (already run): coefficients of albumin, CRP, weight loss, blast %
analyses.result_summary: In adjusted model, all four directions match: albumin +0.122 (p<0.001), crp -0.0065 (p<0.001), weight_loss -0.037 (p<0.001), blast_pct_marrow -0.005 (p<0.001). Joint cachexia/inflammation signal is robust.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS: Treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response (positive direction) specifically in patients with npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0. The other five treatments (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib, 7+3) show no detectable effect on objective_response either as main effects or in mutation-matched subgroups.

analyses.code: Combined subgroup-level Logit(y ~ ven + favorable + ven:favorable) plus stratified per-treatment within-subgroup means
analyses.result_summary: Final ven/aza subgroup effect: +0.628 (78.6% vs 15.8%) within favorable subgroup of n=4531; -0.003 outside (n=45469). Subgroup interaction p<1e-300, three-way interaction p<1e-90. None of the four predicates is dispensable: each violation drops the effect to ~0. Other treatments have no effect within favorable subgroup. This is the best-supported treatment-effect subgroup hypothesis in the cohort.
